headline: A Look At Exxon Mobil (XOM) Valuation After Its Recent Share Price Momentum
summary: Exxon Mobil (XOM) is back in focus after a recent stretch of solid share gains, with the stock up around 9% over the past month and roughly 34% in the past 3 months. headline: Exxon Is Down Since the Iran Conflict. These Energy Stocks Are Bigger Winners.
summary: For all the drama in oil markets since the recent conflict in Iran began, stocks of U.S. oil majors such as  Exxon  Mobil—and even those of many oil producers—haven’t budged much.  In fact, Exxon stock is down from its Friday closing price.  The markets for the fuels those companies produce have been even more volatile than the overall oil market.

headline: Oil Prices Soar to $83 a Barrel. A Potential Energy Shock Is Brewing.
summary: Oil prices climbed higher on Tuesday, extending Monday’s gains as fighting in the Middle East escalated.  Brent crude oil futures  were trading 7.4% higher, at $83.51 a barrel.  West Texas Intermediate  futures were up 6.8% to $76.09.

headline: Oil Prices Jump As Iran Oil-Shock Odds Rise
summary: Oil prices in futures markets hit new crisis highs as the U.S.-Israel war against Iran continued to shut down tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz for a fourth day.  Supply worry boosted the U.S. crude oil price above $77 a barrel, according to CME Group, topping Monday's peak just above $75.  Oil and natural gas stocks accounted for five of the top 10 S&P 500 performers early Tuesday, though nitrogen fertilizer play CF Industries joined the leaders for a similar reason: about one-third of global trade in urea and ammonia goes through the Strait of Hormuz.

headline: Cheniere, Exxon, Chevron Stocks Rise Again. The Energy Shock Is Upending Markets.
summary: Energy stocks were surging ahead of the bell Tuesday, with oil and gas prices soaring as fighting in the Middle East showed no signs of slowing.  Shares in oil exploration and production companies rallied again ahead of the bell—  ConocoPhillips  and  Occidental Petroleum  were both up by more than 3%.  Exxon Mobil  and  Chevron  were also higher.

headline: Oil Prices Top $80 a Barrel. But Natural Gas Is a Big Problem for Markets.
